  • Hide All Code

Cell Painting cpg0016-jump Embedding MAE QC

Michael Tran

2026-04-29

This notebook demonstrates the structure and basic use of the final MultiAssayExperiment containing well-level Cell Painting embeddings, CellProfiler profiles, and compound annotations.

Setup

Load the MAE

mae <- readRDS(mae_path)

assay_names <- names(experiments(mae))
example_assay <- "cpcnn"
embedding_se <- experiments(mae)[[example_assay]]
embedding_mat <- assay(embedding_se)
cellprofiler_se <- experiments(mae)[["cellprofiler"]]
cellprofiler_mat <- assay(cellprofiler_se)

well_metadata <- as.data.frame(colData(mae)) |>
  as_tibble()

compound_metadata <- as.data.frame(metadata(mae)$Drug.Metadata) |>
  as_tibble()

mae
## A MultiAssayExperiment object of 2 listed
##  experiments with user-defined names and respective classes.
##  Containing an ExperimentList class object of length 2:
##  [1] cpcnn: SummarizedExperiment with 672 rows and 793683 columns
##  [2] cellprofiler: SummarizedExperiment with 737 rows and 750114 columns
## Functionality:
##  experiments() - obtain the ExperimentList instance
##  colData() - the primary/phenotype DataFrame
##  sampleMap() - the sample coordination DataFrame
##  `$`, `[`, `[[` - extract colData columns, subset, or experiment
##  *Format() - convert into a long or wide DataFrame
##  assays() - convert ExperimentList to a SimpleList of matrices
##  exportClass() - save data to flat files

Dataset Overview

assay_summary <- tibble(
  assay = assay_names,
  feature_type = case_when(
    assay_names == "cpcnn" ~ "embedding vector positions",
    assay_names == "cellprofiler" ~ "scalar morphology features",
    TRUE ~ "numeric profile features"
  ),
  assay_name = vapply(
    assay_names,
    \(assay_name) {
      paste(assayNames(experiments(mae)[[assay_name]]), collapse = ", ")
    },
    character(1)
  ),
  features = vapply(
    assay_names,
    \(assay_name) nrow(experiments(mae)[[assay_name]]),
    integer(1)
  ),
  wells = vapply(
    assay_names,
    \(assay_name) ncol(experiments(mae)[[assay_name]]),
    integer(1)
  ),
  profile_model = vapply(
    assay_names,
    \(assay_name) {
      paste(
        unique(rowData(experiments(mae)[[assay_name]])$Profile.Model),
        collapse = ", "
      )
    },
    character(1)
  )
)

perturbation_counts <- well_metadata |>
  count(Perturbation.Type, name = "wells", sort = TRUE)

assay_overview_tbl <- bind_rows(
  assay_summary |>
    transmute(
      quantity = paste(assay, "features"),
      value = scales::comma(features)
    ),
  assay_summary |>
    transmute(
      quantity = paste(assay, "wells"),
      value = scales::comma(wells)
    )
)

overview_tbl <- tibble(
  quantity = c(
    "Pipeline ID",
    "Pipeline version",
    "Experiments",
    "Assays",
    "Wells in colData",
    "Plates",
    "Sources",
    "Unique compounds",
    "Drug metadata records",
    "Treatment wells",
    "Negative-control wells",
    "Positive-control wells"
  ),
  value = c(
    metadata(mae)$Pipeline$ID,
    metadata(mae)$Pipeline$Version,
    scales::comma(length(experiments(mae))),
    paste(assay_names, collapse = ", "),
    scales::comma(nrow(well_metadata)),
    scales::comma(n_distinct(well_metadata$Plate)),
    scales::comma(n_distinct(well_metadata$Source)),
    scales::comma(n_distinct(well_metadata$JUMP.CP.ID)),
    scales::comma(nrow(compound_metadata)),
    scales::comma(sum(well_metadata$Perturbation.Type == "treatment")),
    scales::comma(sum(well_metadata$Perturbation.Type == "negcon")),
    scales::comma(sum(well_metadata$Perturbation.Type == "poscon"))
  )
) |>
  bind_rows(assay_overview_tbl)

kable(overview_tbl)
quantity value
Pipeline ID cpg0016_emb
Pipeline version v2.0.0
Experiments 2
Assays cpcnn, cellprofiler
Wells in colData 793,992
Plates 1,729
Sources 10
Unique compounds 115,730
Drug metadata records 115,730
Treatment wells 640,803
Negative-control wells 88,583
Positive-control wells 64,606
cpcnn features 672
cellprofiler features 737
cpcnn wells 793,683
cellprofiler wells 750,114 
kable(assay_summary)
assay feature_type assay_name features wells profile_model
cpcnn embedding vector positions values 672 793683 cpcnn_zenodo_7114558
cellprofiler scalar morphology features values 737 750114 cellprofiler

This file contains 2 assays. The cpcnn assay stores 672 embedding features across 793,683 wells. The cellprofiler assay stores scalar morphology features for the wells where CellProfiler profiles were available.

Inspect the MAE Structure

The object layout is inspected directly below. The experiment container, assay names, well-level metadata, sample map, and object-level metadata can all be accessed from the printed structure and the code shown in this section.

mae
## A MultiAssayExperiment object of 2 listed
##  experiments with user-defined names and respective classes.
##  Containing an ExperimentList class object of length 2:
##  [1] cpcnn: SummarizedExperiment with 672 rows and 793683 columns
##  [2] cellprofiler: SummarizedExperiment with 737 rows and 750114 columns
## Functionality:
##  experiments() - obtain the ExperimentList instance
##  colData() - the primary/phenotype DataFrame
##  sampleMap() - the sample coordination DataFrame
##  `$`, `[`, `[[` - extract colData columns, subset, or experiment
##  *Format() - convert into a long or wide DataFrame
##  assays() - convert ExperimentList to a SimpleList of matrices
##  exportClass() - save data to flat files
experiments(mae)
## ExperimentList class object of length 2:
##  [1] cpcnn: SummarizedExperiment with 672 rows and 793683 columns
##  [2] cellprofiler: SummarizedExperiment with 737 rows and 750114 columns
colnames(colData(mae))
##  [1] "Sample.ID"           "JUMP.CP.ID"          "Pubchem.CID"        
##  [4] "InChIKey"            "JUMP.CP.SMILES"      "In.AnnotationDB"    
##  [7] "AnnotationDB.Name"   "AnnotationDB.SMILES" "Source"             
## [10] "Batch"               "Plate"               "Well"               
## [13] "Perturbation.Type"
head(sampleMap(mae))
## DataFrame with 6 rows and 3 columns
##            assay               primary               colname
##         <factor>           <character>           <character>
## cpcnn.1    cpcnn Dest210607-134443.N19 Dest210607-134443.N19
## cpcnn.2    cpcnn       GR00003282.AC04       GR00003282.AC04
## cpcnn.3    cpcnn      110000295610.N22      110000295610.N22
## cpcnn.4    cpcnn Dest210809-141809.N05 Dest210809-141809.N05
## cpcnn.5    cpcnn      110000295565.N16      110000295565.N16
## cpcnn.6    cpcnn          UL001789.I43          UL001789.I43
names(metadata(mae))
## [1] "Pipeline"      "Drug.Metadata"

Well-Level Metadata

well_metadata |>
  select(
    Sample.ID,
    JUMP.CP.ID,
    Pubchem.CID,
    InChIKey,
    Source,
    Batch,
    Plate,
    Well,
    Perturbation.Type
  ) |>
  slice_head(n = 8) |>
  kable()
Sample.ID JUMP.CP.ID Pubchem.CID InChIKey Source Batch Plate Well Perturbation.Type
Dest210607-134443.N19 JCP2022_002169 110132473 ALIGFVWJHCLCCG-UHFFFAOYSA-N source_10 2021_06_07_U2OS_48_hr_run3 Dest210607-134443 N19 treatment
GR00003282.AC04 JCP2022_090265 75477277 UNBDJSUVRUVXTM-UHFFFAOYSA-N source_9 20211013-Run14 GR00003282 AC04 treatment
110000295610.N22 JCP2022_115320 31326 ZRZNJUXESFHSIO-UHFFFAOYSA-N source_6 p211012CPU2OS48hw384exp033JUMP 110000295610 N22 treatment
Dest210809-141809.N05 JCP2022_000983 30889490 AFDSCABMINXZRC-UHFFFAOYSA-N source_10 2021_08_17_U2OS_48_hr_run16 Dest210809-141809 N05 treatment
110000295565.N16 JCP2022_061641 110251711 NVOQOWPHOZXBRE-UHFFFAOYSA-N source_6 p211004CPU2OS48hw384exp031JUMP 110000295565 N16 treatment
UL001789.I43 JCP2022_022717 46364319 FSUQRPQVCRVLNU-UHFFFAOYSA-N source_1 Batch6_20221102 UL001789 I43 treatment
GR00003386.AE32 JCP2022_064132 875116 OJDWYGXFEFNPEV-UHFFFAOYSA-N source_9 20210824-Run5 GR00003386 AE32 treatment
GR00003390.E17 JCP2022_012837 4667208 CQMZYBFPWYQAGU-UHFFFAOYSA-N source_9 20210825_Run6 GR00003390 E17 treatment

The main fields commonly used in downstream analysis are:

  • Sample.ID: the well identifier used throughout the object. It is the row name of colData(mae) and the column name used by assay matrices.
  • Source: the source-specific study label.
  • Batch: the batch identifier within a source.
  • Plate and Well: the physical plate and well coordinates.
  • JUMP.CP.ID: the perturbagen identifier used to join to compound metadata.
  • InChIKey: the structure-level identifier for the perturbagen.
  • Perturbation.Type: whether the well is a treatment, negative control (negcon), or positive control (poscon).
kable(perturbation_counts)
Perturbation.Type wells
treatment 640803
negcon 88583
poscon 64606 
ggplot(
  perturbation_counts,
  aes(
    x = reorder(Perturbation.Type, wells),
    y = wells,
    fill = Perturbation.Type
  )
) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Well counts by perturbation type",
    x = "Perturbation type",
    y = "Number of wells"
  ) +
  coord_flip()

Treatment compound replication is summarized below. This view focuses on treatments; controls are represented separately in the perturbation summaries.

compound_replicates <- well_metadata |>
  filter(Perturbation.Type == "treatment") |>
  count(JUMP.CP.ID, name = "wells", sort = TRUE) |>
  left_join(
    compound_metadata |>
      select(
        JUMP.CP.ID,
        AnnotationDB.Name,
        Pubchem.CID,
        InChIKey,
        Control.Name
      ),
    by = "JUMP.CP.ID"
  )

replicate_summary_tbl <- tibble(
  quantity = c(
    "Treatment compounds",
    "Treatment compounds with more than one well",
    "Median wells per treatment compound",
    "95th percentile wells per treatment compound",
    "Maximum wells for one treatment compound"
  ),
  value = c(
    scales::comma(nrow(compound_replicates)),
    scales::comma(sum(compound_replicates$wells > 1)),
    scales::comma(median(compound_replicates$wells)),
    scales::comma(quantile(compound_replicates$wells, 0.95, names = FALSE)),
    scales::comma(max(compound_replicates$wells))
  )
)

kable(replicate_summary_tbl)
quantity value
Treatment compounds 115,720
Treatment compounds with more than one well 115,719
Median wells per treatment compound 5
95th percentile wells per treatment compound 9
Maximum wells for one treatment compound 58 
ggplot(
  compound_replicates,
  aes(x = wells)
) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 45, fill = "#3B6EA8", color = "white") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Treatment wells per compound",
    subtitle = "Controls are excluded by perturbation type.",
    x = "Wells per treatment compound",
    y = "Compounds"
  )

Inspect the CPCNN Assay

kable(head(as.data.frame(rowData(embedding_se)), 3))
Feature.ID Feature.Source Feature.Index Profile.Model Assay.Name
all_emb_0001 all_emb_0001 all_emb 1 cpcnn_zenodo_7114558 cpcnn
all_emb_0002 all_emb_0002 all_emb 2 cpcnn_zenodo_7114558 cpcnn
all_emb_0003 all_emb_0003 all_emb 3 cpcnn_zenodo_7114558 cpcnn 
dim(rowData(embedding_se))
## [1] 672   5

The cpcnn experiment is a SummarizedExperiment with one assay called values. Rows are embedding features and columns are wells.

tibble(
  metric = c("Embedding features per well", "Wells in assay"),
  value = c(nrow(embedding_mat), ncol(embedding_mat))
) |>
  kable()
metric value
Embedding features per well 672
Wells in assay 793683

The UMAP below is a visualization of a reproducible sample of 1,000 treatment wells from the cpcnn assay. Each point is one well-level profile.

set.seed(20260429)

cpcnn_treatment_wells <- well_metadata |>
  filter(
    Perturbation.Type == "treatment",
    Sample.ID %in% colnames(embedding_mat)
  ) |>
  left_join(
    compound_metadata |>
      select(JUMP.CP.ID, Control.Name),
    by = "JUMP.CP.ID"
  ) |>
  filter(is.na(Control.Name))

umap_sample_n <- min(1000L, nrow(cpcnn_treatment_wells))

umap_wells <- cpcnn_treatment_wells |>
  slice_sample(n = umap_sample_n)

umap_embeddings <- embedding_mat[, umap_wells$Sample.ID, drop = FALSE]

umap_input <- scale(t(umap_embeddings))
umap_input[is.na(umap_input)] <- 0

umap_layout <- uwot::umap(
  umap_input,
  n_neighbors = 15,
  min_dist = 0.2,
  metric = "cosine",
  n_threads = 1,
  n_sgd_threads = 1,
  verbose = FALSE
)

umap_tbl <- as_tibble(umap_layout, .name_repair = \(x) c("UMAP1", "UMAP2")) |>
  bind_cols(
    umap_wells |>
      select(Sample.ID, JUMP.CP.ID, Source, Plate, Well)
  )

ggplot(
  umap_tbl,
  aes(x = UMAP1, y = UMAP2, color = Source)
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, size = 1.3) +
  labs(
    title = "UMAP of sampled cpcnn well profiles",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Well-level embeddings for ",
      scales::comma(nrow(umap_tbl)),
      " sampled treatment wells; controls excluded."
    ),
    x = "UMAP 1",
    y = "UMAP 2",
    color = "Source"
  )

embedding_mat[1:6, 1:4]
##              Dest210607-134443.N19 GR00003282.AC04 110000295610.N22
## all_emb_0001             1.7471794       0.9005039         1.455983
## all_emb_0002             2.5285974       2.6223581         2.704039
## all_emb_0003             1.4317852       2.1683698         1.120903
## all_emb_0004             0.8901504       1.6842115         1.149817
## all_emb_0005             1.0509490       2.5887990         1.653135
## all_emb_0006             0.8935499       1.5391492         1.083898
##              Dest210809-141809.N05
## all_emb_0001             1.4962467
## all_emb_0002             2.1586897
## all_emb_0003             2.9967451
## all_emb_0004             0.6773452
## all_emb_0005             1.1195399
## all_emb_0006             0.9702294

The cpcnn assay columns are represented in colData(mae).

all(colnames(embedding_mat) %in% well_metadata$Sample.ID)
## [1] TRUE

Inspect the CellProfiler Assay

The cellprofiler experiment has the same SummarizedExperiment layout as cpcnn: one values assay with features as rows and wells as columns. The main difference is feature meaning. cpcnn rows are embedding vector positions, while cellprofiler rows are scalar morphology features from the CellProfiler profile table.

kable(head(as.data.frame(rowData(cellprofiler_se)), 8))
Feature.ID Feature.Source Feature.Index Profile.Model Assay.Name
X_1 X_1 X_1 1 cellprofiler cellprofiler
X_2 X_2 X_2 1 cellprofiler cellprofiler
X_3 X_3 X_3 1 cellprofiler cellprofiler
X_4 X_4 X_4 1 cellprofiler cellprofiler
X_5 X_5 X_5 1 cellprofiler cellprofiler
X_6 X_6 X_6 1 cellprofiler cellprofiler
X_7 X_7 X_7 1 cellprofiler cellprofiler
X_8 X_8 X_8 1 cellprofiler cellprofiler 
tibble(
  metric = c(
    "CellProfiler features per well",
    "CellProfiler wells in assay",
    "CellProfiler wells represented in colData",
    "CellProfiler wells not in colData"
  ),
  value = c(
    nrow(cellprofiler_mat),
    ncol(cellprofiler_mat),
    sum(colnames(cellprofiler_mat) %in% well_metadata$Sample.ID),
    sum(!(colnames(cellprofiler_mat) %in% well_metadata$Sample.ID))
  )
) |>
  kable()
metric value
CellProfiler features per well 737
CellProfiler wells in assay 750114
CellProfiler wells represented in colData 750114
CellProfiler wells not in colData 0 
cellprofiler_mat[1:6, 1:4]

## X_1      0.9073636      1.0564263    -1.11075437   -0.7961960
## X_2      0.8933774     -0.1732340     0.36920089    0.1567714
## X_3     -0.2657616     -0.1964321     0.02818934   -0.7304401
## X_4      0.0767049      1.0175720    -0.96340978   -0.9040873
## X_5     -0.5475310     -0.4958429     0.87367624   -0.2574983
## X_6     -0.9669507      1.2100601    -0.69021785    0.6940953

Compound Metadata

compound_preview <- compound_metadata |>
  select(
    JUMP.CP.ID,
    InChIKey,
    Perturbation.Type,
    AnnotationDB.Name,
    Pubchem.CID,
    In.AnnotationDB
  ) |>
  slice_head(n = 8)

kable(compound_preview)
JUMP.CP.ID InChIKey Perturbation.Type AnnotationDB.Name Pubchem.CID In.AnnotationDB
JCP2022_000001 AAAHWCWPZPSPIW-UHFFFAOYSA-N treatment [4-[2-(Dimethylamino)ethoxy]phenyl]-[2-[6-(2-ethylimidazol-1-yl)-2-pyridinyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]methanone 110094592 TRUE
JCP2022_000002 AAAJHRMBUHXWLD-UHFFFAOYSA-N treatment 1-(4-chlorobenzyl)tetrahydropyrimidin-2(1H)-one 5076487 TRUE
JCP2022_000004 AAANUZMCJQUYNX-UHFFFAOYSA-N treatment NA NA FALSE
JCP2022_000005 AAAQFGUYHFJNHI-UHFFFAOYSA-N treatment 4H-[1,2,4]Triazolo[4,3-a][1,4]benzodiazepine-4-acetamide, 6-(4-chlorophenyl)-N-ethyl-8-methoxy-1-methyl- 52934829 TRUE
JCP2022_000006 AAAROXVLYNJINN-UHFFFAOYSA-N treatment 5-(1-Tert-butyl-5-cyclopropylpyrazol-3-yl)-3-(1-methylpyrazol-4-yl)-1,2,4-oxadiazole 72019641 TRUE
JCP2022_000007 AAATVJFGQAZXQO-UHFFFAOYSA-N treatment [1-(1H-indol-3-yl)-1-oxopropan-2-yl] 2-[(3,5-dimethyl-1,2-oxazol-4-yl)methylsulfanyl]benzoate 16324273 TRUE
JCP2022_000008 AAAUIKUIDRZZQT-UHFFFAOYSA-N treatment N-(3-chlorophenyl)-2-(3-methyl-2-oxo-5-phenyl-2,3-dihydro-1H-1,4-benzodiazepin-1-yl)butanamide 15998487 TRUE
JCP2022_000009 AAAZRMGPBSWFDK-UHFFFAOYSA-N treatment 2-(2-(4-(2-(Trifluoromethyl)phenylsulfonyl)piperazin-1-yl)acetamido)thiophene-3-carboxamide 16231644 TRUE

This table is stored once at the object level instead of being repeated on every well. The join key is JUMP.CP.ID.

The Perturbation.Type labels also appear in the compound metadata. In this context, negcon denotes negative controls and poscon denotes positive controls.

For compounds represented in the cpcnn assay, most records are enriched with AnnotationDB names and identifiers. Some compounds, such as JCP2022_000004, do not have mapped AnnotationDB names, PubChem CIDs, or AnnotationDB SMILES. The overall breakdown is shown below.

cpcnn_compound_metadata <- well_metadata |>
  filter(Sample.ID %in% colnames(embedding_mat)) |>
  distinct(JUMP.CP.ID) |>
  left_join(compound_metadata, by = "JUMP.CP.ID")

annotation_availability <- cpcnn_compound_metadata |>
  mutate(
    annotation_status = if_else(
      In.AnnotationDB,
      "AnnotationDB enriched",
      "No AnnotationDB match",
      missing = "No AnnotationDB match"
    )
  ) |>
  count(annotation_status, name = "compounds") |>
  mutate(
    fraction = compounds / sum(compounds),
    percent = scales::percent(fraction, accuracy = 0.1),
    label = paste0(
      annotation_status,
      "\n",
      scales::comma(compounds),
      " (",
      percent,
      ")"
    )
  )

kable(
  annotation_availability |>
    select(annotation_status, compounds, percent)
)
annotation_status compounds percent
AnnotationDB enriched 106566 92.1%
No AnnotationDB match 9164 7.9% 
ggplot(
  annotation_availability,
  aes(x = "", y = fraction, fill = annotation_status)
) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = label),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    lineheight = 0.95
  ) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#4C956C", "#C75C5C")) +
  labs(
    title = "AnnotationDB metadata availability for cpcnn compounds",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_void()

negcon_examples <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(Perturbation.Type == "negcon") |>
  select(
    JUMP.CP.ID,
    Control.Name,
    AnnotationDB.Name,
    Pubchem.CID
  ) |>
  slice_head(n = 3)

poscon_examples <- compound_metadata |>
  filter(Perturbation.Type == "poscon") |>
  arrange(desc(JUMP.CP.ID)) |>
  select(
    JUMP.CP.ID,
    Control.Name,
    AnnotationDB.Name,
    Pubchem.CID
  ) |>
  slice_head(n = 3)

kable(negcon_examples, caption = "Example negative-control compounds")
Example negative-control compounds
JUMP.CP.ID Control.Name AnnotationDB.Name Pubchem.CID
JCP2022_033924 DMSO Dimethyl Sulfoxide 679 
kable(poscon_examples, caption = "Example positive-control compounds")
Example positive-control compounds
JUMP.CP.ID Control.Name AnnotationDB.Name Pubchem.CID
JCP2022_085227 aloxistatin Ethyl epoxysuccinyl-leucylamido-3-methylbutane 3189
JCP2022_064022 NVS-PAK1-1 3-((2-Chloro-5-(2,2-difluoroethyl)-8-fluoro-5H-dibenzo[b,e][1,4]diazepin-11-yl)amino)-N-isopropylpyrrolidine-1-carboxamide 138115195
JCP2022_050797 quinidine Cinchonan-9-ol, 6’-methoxy-, (8alpha,9R)- 1065

Demonstrate Basic Access Patterns

Pull One Plate and Its Embeddings

plate_id <- well_metadata$Plate[1]

plate_wells <- well_metadata |>
  filter(Plate == plate_id, Sample.ID %in% colnames(embedding_mat))

plate_embeddings <- embedding_mat[, plate_wells$Sample.ID, drop = FALSE]

kable(
  plate_wells |>
    select(
      Sample.ID,
      Well,
      JUMP.CP.ID,
      Perturbation.Type
    ) |>
    slice_head(n = 8)
)
Sample.ID Well JUMP.CP.ID Perturbation.Type
Dest210607-134443.N19 N19 JCP2022_002169 treatment
Dest210607-134443.G20 G20 JCP2022_093263 treatment
Dest210607-134443.H04 H04 JCP2022_095326 treatment
Dest210607-134443.O14 O14 JCP2022_037505 treatment
Dest210607-134443.F24 F24 JCP2022_037716 poscon
Dest210607-134443.K17 K17 JCP2022_019618 treatment
Dest210607-134443.I06 I06 JCP2022_019219 treatment
Dest210607-134443.C15 C15 JCP2022_037886 treatment 
dim(plate_embeddings)
## [1] 672 382

Annotate Wells with Compound Metadata

plate_treatment_wells <- plate_wells |>
  filter(Perturbation.Type == "treatment")

plate_treatment_annotated <- plate_treatment_wells |>
  select(
    Sample.ID,
    Source,
    Plate,
    Well,
    JUMP.CP.ID
  ) |>
  left_join(
    compound_metadata |>
      select(
        JUMP.CP.ID,
        AnnotationDB.Name,
        Pubchem.CID,
        In.AnnotationDB
      ),
    by = "JUMP.CP.ID"
  )

kable(slice_head(plate_treatment_annotated, n = 8))
Sample.ID Source Plate Well JUMP.CP.ID AnnotationDB.Name Pubchem.CID In.AnnotationDB

Dest210607-134443.C15 source_10 Dest210607-134443 C15 JCP2022_037886 1-(3-chlorophenyl)-3-((6-(1-methyl-1H-pyrazol-5-yl)pyridin-3-yl)methyl)urea 121023442 TRUE
Dest210607-134443.D03 source_10 Dest210607-134443 D03 JCP2022_051500 2-(4-Chlorophenoxy)-2-methyl-1-[4-(thiolan-3-yl)-1,4-diazepan-1-yl]propan-1-one 134160972 TRUE

The same join pattern works for any well subset:

treatment_wells <- well_metadata |>
  filter(
    Perturbation.Type == "treatment",
    Sample.ID %in% colnames(embedding_mat)
  )

treatment_embeddings <- embedding_mat[, treatment_wells$Sample.ID, drop = FALSE]

treatment_annotations <- treatment_wells |>
  left_join(
    compound_metadata,
    by = "JUMP.CP.ID"
  )

Extract Embeddings for a Small Well Set

example_wells <- plate_treatment_wells$Sample.ID[seq_len(min(
  4,
  nrow(plate_treatment_wells)
))]

embedding_mat[1:10, example_wells, drop = FALSE]
##              Dest210607-134443.N19 Dest210607-134443.G20 Dest210607-134443.H04
## all_emb_0001             1.7471794             1.5646025             1.6318797
## all_emb_0002             2.5285974             2.4691734             2.5115931
## all_emb_0003             1.4317852             1.5929955             1.4785525
## all_emb_0004             0.8901504             0.9677522             0.8726900
## all_emb_0005             1.0509490             1.0516798             1.1063930
## all_emb_0006             0.8935499             0.8201179             0.9844046
## all_emb_0007             1.6671696             1.3333355             1.6165138
## all_emb_0008             0.7661944             1.1351831             1.0195005
## all_emb_0009             1.0824745             1.1245739             1.0411267
## all_emb_0010             0.7689299             0.7754836             0.7757193
##              Dest210607-134443.O14
## all_emb_0001             1.7203033
## all_emb_0002             2.6052914
## all_emb_0003             1.4772871
## all_emb_0004             0.9501830
## all_emb_0005             1.1200948
## all_emb_0006             0.8205119
## all_emb_0007             1.9542652
## all_emb_0008             0.5749847
## all_emb_0009             1.1315907
## all_emb_0010             0.8126057

This gives a small feature-by-well slice. For modeling or distance calculations, the wells are typically subset first and the result is then kept as a matrix.

Build One Mean Profile Per Compound

treatment_wells <- well_metadata |>
  filter(
    Perturbation.Type == "treatment",
    Sample.ID %in% colnames(embedding_mat)
  )

example_compounds <- treatment_wells |>
  count(JUMP.CP.ID, name = "n_wells", sort = TRUE) |>
  filter(n_wells > 1) |>
  slice_head(n = 5)

example_treatment_wells <- treatment_wells |>
  semi_join(example_compounds, by = "JUMP.CP.ID")

treatment_embeddings <- embedding_mat[,
  example_treatment_wells$Sample.ID,
  drop = FALSE
]

compound_means <- rowsum(
  x = t(treatment_embeddings),
  group = example_treatment_wells$JUMP.CP.ID,
  reorder = FALSE
)

compound_counts <- table(example_treatment_wells$JUMP.CP.ID)

compound_means <- compound_means /
  as.numeric(compound_counts[rownames(compound_means)])

compound_mean_preview <- as.data.frame(
  compound_means[, seq_len(min(6, ncol(compound_means))), drop = FALSE],
  check.names = FALSE
) |>
  rownames_to_column("JUMP.CP.ID") |>
  as_tibble() |>
  left_join(example_compounds, by = "JUMP.CP.ID") |>
  select(JUMP.CP.ID, n_wells, everything())

kable(compound_mean_preview)
JUMP.CP.ID n_wells all_emb_0001 all_emb_0002 all_emb_0003 all_emb_0004 all_emb_0005 all_emb_0006
JCP2022_109350 49 1.089930 2.499555 2.354518 1.434030 1.480916 0.7527553
JCP2022_101934 58 1.272925 2.511254 2.199807 1.440963 1.485834 0.7696769
JCP2022_018628 47 1.131040 2.486283 2.440456 1.597452 1.544299 0.6941598
JCP2022_072565 41 1.071003 2.490514 2.578724 1.625687 1.563458 0.6468159
JCP2022_050997 43 1.245778 2.509316 2.164143 1.424489 1.450678 0.7753809 
dim(compound_means)
## [1]   5 672

This preview averages the cpcnn embeddings for a few replicated treatment compounds. After this step, compound_means has one row per compound and one column per embedding feature.
